records.title: Intraday fallback execution parity
records.statement: app/api/zl/intraday must execute the declared 15m->1m fallback behavior when primary query returns no usable bars.
acceptance_criteria: Route contains an executable fallback query to mkt.price_1m when mkt.price_15m is unusable.
Response source metadata reflects the table actually used.
Fallback behavior is mechanically testable by source-level contract assertions.

records.title: Forecast-target envelope parity
records.statement: Forecast target endpoints must expose one stable envelope contract for equivalent semantic payloads.
acceptance_criteria: Forecast-target routes emit a canonical envelope or publish explicit typed adapters used by all consumers.
Success and error payload semantics are shared across equivalent domain endpoints.
Horizon normalization behavior remains equivalent across forecast-facing routes.

records.title: Canonical ops.ingest_run status vocabulary
records.statement: All writers to ops.ingest_run.status must share one canonical status vocabulary.
acceptance_criteria: SQL and TS writers use one status enum/value set.
Lifecycle rows are comparable without producer-specific translation.
Terminal success/failure statuses are normalized across writer paths.

records.title: Explicit approval gate for training and promotion
records.statement: Training and cloud promotion execution requires explicit approval in non-dry runs.
acceptance_criteria: Non-dry train path blocks execution when approved flag is false.
Non-dry promote path blocks execution when approved flag is false.
Approval flags propagate unchanged through pipeline dispatch.

records.title: Runtime external pull resilience on AI-first routes
records.functional_section: AI Snapshot and Runtime Source Resilience
records.statement: User-facing AI-first routes that perform runtime trusted-market pulls must preserve explicit degraded-mode behavior and fallback metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Routes preserve explicit fallback behavior when runtime pulls fail.
Responses expose provenance metadata sufficient to identify degraded mode.
Pull failures do not return opaque payloads that appear fully fresh.

records.title: Operator documentation parity with authority rules
records.statement: Operator-facing docs must not contain startup guidance that conflicts with V16 authority rules, including hardcoded port assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: Root docs avoid hardcoded localhost:3000 startup assumptions.
Onboarding docs remain consistent with authority startup constraints.

records.title: Per-bug writeup artifact location
records.statement: Per-bug writeups are placed at quality/writeups/BUG-<id>.md.
acceptance_criteria: Each confirmed bug has one writeup file under quality/writeups with BUG-<id>.md naming.

records.title: Fix patch artifact location
records.statement: Fix patches are placed at quality/patches/BUG-<id>-fix.patch.
acceptance_criteria: Each fix patch artifact resides in quality/patches using BUG-<id>-fix.patch naming.

records.title: Regression-test patch artifact location
acceptance_criteria: Each regression-test patch artifact resides in quality/patches using BUG-<id>-regression-test.patch naming.

records.title: Code review output artifact location
records.statement: Code review output is placed at quality/code_reviews/.
acceptance_criteria: Code review reports are saved under quality/code_reviews/.

records.title: Spec audit output artifact location
records.statement: Spec audit outputs (auditor reports + triage) are placed at quality/spec_audits/.
acceptance_criteria: Auditor reports and triage outputs are saved under quality/spec_audits/.

records.title: Sidecar JSON artifact location
records.statement: Sidecar JSON results (tdd-results.json, integration-results.json, recheck-results.json) are placed at quality/results/.
acceptance_criteria: Sidecar JSON result files are saved under quality/results/.

records.title: Mechanical-verification artifact location
records.statement: Mechanical-verification artifacts (verify.sh + *_cases.txt) are placed at quality/mechanical/.
acceptance_criteria: quality/mechanical/verify.sh exists and executes successfully.
All case extraction artifacts are colocated in quality/mechanical/.

records.title: Forbidden workspace subtree
records.statement: quality/workspace/<name>/ is forbidden — top-level quality/<name>/ is the only canonical layout. Phase 6 gate check_no_workspace_dir fails any run with a quality/workspace/ tree present (populated OR empty — empty workspace/ trains future-iteration agents on the wrong layout and is rejected too).
acceptance_criteria: No quality/workspace directory exists.
All generated artifacts are stored directly under top-level quality subdirectories.
